mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and compress equity valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS exceeds +0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of rising rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and impending recession, causing investors to discount future earnings and driving negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue expansion and producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS yields negative 60-day forward returns in >55% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, triggering short-term momentum buying that pushes S&P 500 returns positive over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns in >70% of early-cycle observations, or autocorrelation dominates the signal.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly inflation spike above 0.4% forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of triggered inflation spikes, or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms a soft landing environment, sustaining consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from independent PAYEMS trends.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts reflect strong household balance sheets and construction sector activity, leading to positive equity market momentum over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST levels show no predictive power for 20-day returns (Rank IC < 0.02) across multiple market cycles, or returns are negative in >65% of high HOUST periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid industrial production growth occurring alongside elevated federal funds rates signals economic overheating and impending monetary restriction, triggering short-term profit-taking and negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns in >60% of overheating periods, or the interaction term loses significance after adjusting for market volatility.
